docs.title: Home
docs.text:
textfsm-ai is a modern, Pythonic engine for parsing unstructured text using TextFSM templates — with a clean API, strong validation, and developer‑friendly tooling.

It is designed for:

  • network engineers automating CLI parsing
  • developers building structured extractors
  • teams maintaining large template libraries
  • anyone who wants predictable, testable text parsing

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• 🚀 Fast, deterministic parsing
  • 🧪 Golden tests for template validation
  • 🧰 Clean Python API and CLI
  • 📦 Zero external dependencies
  • 📚 Full versioned documentation
  • 🔍 Helpful error messages and template diagnostics

docs.title: Documentation
docs.text:
• Latest docs: https://geeks-trident-llc.github.io/textfsm-ai/latest/
  • All versions: https://geeks-trident-llc.github.io/textfsm-ai/

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: pip install textfsm-ai

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

The format follows Keep a Changelog and this project adheres to Semantic Versioning.

docs.title: Added
docs.text: • Initial public release

docs.title: CLI Guide
docs.text: textfsm-ai includes a simple, powerful CLI for validating templates and parsing text.

docs.title: textfsm-ai providers list
docs.text: List all registered provider types.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: textfsm-ai providers list

docs.title: Example output
docs.text:
openai      (OpenAIProvider)
anthropic   (AnthropicProvider)
gemini      (GeminiProvider)
azure       (AzureOpenAIProvider)

#### `docs/cli/providers-test.md`

```markdown
# `textfsm-ai providers test`

Send a test prompt through the orchestrator to the appropriate provider.

## Usage

```bash
textfsm-ai providers test \
  --model openai/gpt-4o-mini \
  --prompt "Say hello"

Optional:

textfsm-ai providers test \
  --config ./textfsm_ai.yaml \
  --model anthropic/claude-3-5-sonnet \
  --prompt "Summarize this CLI output"

#### `docs/cli/providers-info.md`

```markdown
# `textfsm-ai providers info`

Show configuration-related info for a provider (safe fields only).

## Usage

```bash
textfsm-ai providers info --name openai

Example output:

Name: openai
Type: openai
Params: {'api_key': '***'}

#### `docs/cli/orchestrator-route.md`

```markdown
# `textfsm-ai orchestrator route`

Show which provider a model would be routed to.

## Usage

```bash
textfsm-ai orchestrator route --model anthropic/claude-3-5-sonnet

Example output:

Model: anthropic/claude-3-5-sonnet
Routed provider: anthropic

#### `docs/cli/orchestrator-run.md`

```markdown
# `textfsm-ai orchestrator run`

Run a full orchestrator request and print the response.

## Usage

```bash
textfsm-ai orchestrator run \
  --model gemini/gemini-1.5-flash \
  --prompt "Extract fields from this CLI output"

Optional config file:

textfsm-ai orchestrator run \
  --config ./textfsm_ai.yaml \
  --model openai/gpt-4o-mini \
  --prompt "Generate a TextFSM template"

---

### 6. Example commands for users

```bash
# List providers
textfsm-ai providers list

# Show info for a configured provider
textfsm-ai providers info --name openai

# Test a provider via env-based config
export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
textfsm-ai providers test \
  --model openai/gpt-4o-mini \
  --prompt "Say hello"

# Test routing only
textfsm-ai orchestrator route --model anthropic/claude-3-5-sonnet

# Run full orchestrator call
textfsm-ai orchestrator run \
  --model gemini/gemini-1.5-flash \
  --prompt "Extract fields from this CLI output"

docs.title: Installation
docs.text: textfsm-ai supports Python 3.9+ and works on all major platforms.

docs.title: Install from PyPI
docs.text: pip install textfsm-ai

docs.title: Verify installation
docs.text: python -c "import textfsm_ai; print(textfsm_ai.__version__)"

docs.title: Optional: Install development tools
docs.text:
pip install -r requirements-dev.txt

This includes:

  • mkdocs + mkdocstrings
  • pytest
  • golden test utilities ```

docs.title: Quickstart
docs.text: This guide shows how to parse text using a TextFSM template.

docs.title: Golden Tests
docs.text:
Golden tests ensure your TextFSM templates remain stable and correct over time.

A golden test consists of:

  • a template (.template)
  • an input text file (.txt)
  • an expected output file (.json)

docs.title: Directory structure
docs.text:
tests/golden/
  show_ip_interface/
    template
    input.txt
    expected.json

docs.title: Run all golden tests
docs.text: pytest -k golden

docs.title: Why golden tests?
docs.text:
• Prevent regressions
  • Ensure template stability
  • Document expected behavior
  • Provide fast feedback during development

docs.title: Writing a new golden test
docs.text:
1. Create a folder under tests/golden/
  2. Add:
  3. template
  4. input.txt
  5. expected.json
  6. Run:
pytest -k golden --update

docs.title: OpenAI provider
docs.text: Prefix: openai/

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Environment variables:

  • OPENAI_API_KEY

YAML:

providers:
  - name: openai
    type: openai
    params:
      api_key: ${OPENAI_API_KEY}

docs.title: Example models
docs.text:
• openai/gpt-4o-mini
  • openai/gpt-4o

docs.title: Example usage
docs.text:
textfsm-ai providers test \
  --model openai/gpt-4o-mini \
  --prompt "Extract fields from this CLI output"
```

Repeat similarly for:

  • docs/providers/anthropic.md
  • docs/providers/gemini.md
  • docs/providers/azure.md

Each with:

  • prefix
  • required env vars
  • example YAML config
  • example models
  • example CLI command
